storyInfo: Zuo Le and Mo Yi exchange pointed words in the Sky Mirror Pavilion, as the Sui Regulator's secrets begin to reveal themselves. Ch'en and Ch'en Chao-ch'ien talk about the dark history of the Ch'en family. They begin to unearth the hidden truth.

attributes.content: <p=2>Two days before the day of the full moon, Hour of Hai</>
attributes.name: group_location_stamp

attributes.content: The man walks past rows of fallen bookshelves, trying to return the scattered books to where they should be.

'The Earth's blessings have granted this stone to its people; the Heaven's blessings shall bring an end to Sui'... but in this thousand-year river of history, is it fate ordained by heaven and earth, or nothing more than a coincidence?
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: Lord Zuo and Miss Ch'en...
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: It was they who brought the token to the Sky Mirror Pavilion today...
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: Was it a coincidence, or...
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: This mayhem... what happened?
Someone joins him, stepping lightly, and bends down to pick up a book from the ground.
Mr. Liang.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: Miss Ning... why are you still here?
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: Did you forget that I am an official of the Ministry of Rites? Neither of us should leave, until all the civilians have been evacuated.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: You are quite right...
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: Is the evacuation of the civilians complete?
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: Essentially. Baizao will soon separate.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: Tis unsettling... is it not?
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: Perhaps you wanted to ask how I knew you were here, when you did not tell me about your demotion.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: ...It rained through the night yesterday. I only fear that you may be cold...
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: The man clears a little spot for the two of them to sit down.
It has been a long time since I last visited the Sky Mirror Pavilion.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: I suppose you used to frequent here?
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: As did all the students of the Academy.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: I had a headache whenever I looked up at the books stacked all the way up to the ceiling.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: But now... I find here a place of rare peace, away from the troubles beyond.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: I... I heard about Mr. Ning.
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: You have my deepest sympathies....
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: My grandfather did what he felt was right.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: He would not have kept the secret for so long out of selfish interests. He did it to protect the younger generations too.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: I cannot reproach him, whether with reason or sentiment.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: Of course, he must also pay the price for his deeds.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: I see you have laid it down... I am glad to see it.
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: Perhaps you misunderstand. I do not mean that I could lay it down easily.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: I cannot deny that my career benefited greatly from my grandfather's influence... those on the outside would undoubtedly say that the House of Ning has fallen on hard times.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: I would not say this to anyone but you, Mr. Liang.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: You should not say that. I do not feel that any of your honors are undeserved.
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: I trust you—
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: What if you are the only one?
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: My grandfather said that once one has passed the walls of the palace, one must not trust in others easily, and certainly not expect to be trusted easily.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: I listened... but it seems that I forgot about it when I met you in Shangshu.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: I simply believed that I found a kindred spirit.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: You... you flatter me.
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: You've always been like this.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: I admire your honesty and your sense of responsibility, but I hate how you make decisions by yourself and keep other people at a distance.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: But then I realized... you would not be Liang Xun, if everything you did was to my liking.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: I could no more reproach you than I could reproach my grandfather.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: I once thought that all I needed to do was to be fair, honest, diligent, and persevering.
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: But the years have taught me how powerless I am.
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: Your grandfather was right, but even more frightening is the thought that I could not even trust myself to keep my aspirations and ideals.
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: I would not claim that everything I did was honest and upright... but you should not have been involved in the ones that were not.
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: Is it my turn to comfort you with the words 'clear conscience'?
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: You and I have done all we could, following the path of justice that we believed in.
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: Perhaps we are ultimately powerless to change anything. Perhaps this entire city will be no more when the sun rises tomorrow...
attributes.name: Ning Ciqiu

attributes.content: Then... here and now, at least... my conscience is clear.
attributes.name: Liang Xun

attributes.content: The night is deep.
The ancient library has kept some old rules. All is quiet, except the passage of time.
They hold each other's hands tightly in their own.
The desolate pond lies wrinkled by the wind; beneath the crumbling eaves, no voices remain. One can still recall those days of green sashes and red plumes in their splendor...
<p=2>One day before the day of the full moon, Hour of Tzu</>
attributes.name: group_location_stamp

attributes.content: The scriptgeists seem to be appearing less frequently.
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Could it be that you were right, and these geists are the breathing of the bestial Sui...?
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: I told you so. You just won't believe me.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Whatever the reason, this is our opportunity to head for the top—
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Hm... so this is the Sky Mirror Pavilion's restricted area.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: What are you planning?
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: These geists turn back into words when they're defeated.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Say, if they were transformed from the secret tomes of the Sky Mirror Pavilion, would defeating them reveal the secrets of Yan that are usually kept hidden?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: You dare—?
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: No sudden moves!
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Let me see what secrets the words form!
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: 'Sang' 'Tu' 'Cui' 'Shou'... what is this? It makes no sense.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: The top three floors of the Sky Mirror Pavilion are off-limits. We only enter because we have an important mission.
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Refrain from doing anything unnecessary.
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Hah. You still won't admit that you're already in on this.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Your deceptive words mean nothing. I know what I'm doing...
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: There's a lot that you don't know.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: This building began as a public library, one of the first buildings to be completed after Baizao became a nomadic city. But there was no such thing as a restricted area at the time.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Then, one hundred and twenty years ago, the death of the proxy affected tons of history books, causing their text to become unreadable.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: The Sui Regulator wanted to dig deep, but the number of books was enormous, so the Sky Mirror Pavilion set aside space up top for the Sui Regulator's research.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Decades passed. When the discussion of the turmoil had almost settled, the place became a warehouse for dusty old books.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Some restricted zone. Why would it be sealed off from public view, if it held any actual knowledge?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: You do know a lot...
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Just a little bit more than you.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: The things you speak of should not be secrets to the Sui Regulator. But I have not heard them before.
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: My studies have been inadequate.
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: You—this is what I hate about you. Always stiff, always by the book.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Was that white-haired Perro we met in Mangshan Town a friend of yours?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: You mean... Yun Qingping?
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Yeah, that's the name, I think.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: He couldn't fight his way out of a wet paper bag, and it looked like a stiff wind could blow him over, but he was a quick thinker. Talking to him was kind of interesting.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Why don't you learn a thing or two from him?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: I know Yun's smart! I don't need you to lecture me!
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Hahaha! Now you start to drop the official tone and sound like a human being.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: ...Why did you join the Shanhaizhong, Mo Yi?
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: What answer do you want to hear?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: You want me to tell you that I had a difficult childhood, drifted into the Shanhaizhong, and I'm looking for a chance to repent and mend my ways?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Doesn't that sound a little cliche?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: I wasn't—
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Say whatever you want.
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Of course, the Shanhaizhong who murder and pillage are villains. They deserve to die.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: But I hate hypocrites even more, those people who look like they'll die if they stopped flaunting their high and mighty morals for a single moment.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: They think they're better than everyone else the moment they come out of their mama's tummy. Frauds!
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: What I really want to know is... why are you so sure that this thing from the Garden of Grotesqueries will stop a war between Yan and Sui?
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: You've been with the Sui Regulator for a while. Have you never wondered about their most closely-guarded secret...?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Why did that proxy die?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Looks like it's not that you're not curious, it's that curiosity has yielded no answers.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: That's the problem, isn't it? Why would this have become a secret, if the Sui Regulator was really so just and upright?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: What's the matter?
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: No, it's nothing...
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Did you not hear any of that?
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: You're so weird. You've been staring into blank space for a while now.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Did those geists give you a concussion? Don't suppose there's anything I can do about that.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Thanks. I'm not quite—
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: I just feel like...
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: I've been here before...
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: You said you've dreamt about this place before. Were you dreaming just now?
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Perhaps.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: I thought I heard two people arguing... something about 'justice', but I'm not sure...
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: You must be hearing things. It's mostly ancient history books here. Maybe there's a clue as to what's going on here, but justice? That's one thing you won't find.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: They seemed to be saying that justice doesn't exist, tangibly...
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: That's true enough.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Never mind that. Let's get searching. Maybe you'll find your father's books here.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: His books, here?
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Talk about forgetting one's roots.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: But I suppose it's not your fault. You didn't grow up in Baizao, and these things didn't really have anything to do with you.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: I thought you would be angrier at me.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: I am angry, but I realize I shouldn't take it out on you.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: I just... never mind.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Miscellaneous Morsels from Baizao Suiyang... yes, this one dates back several generations.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: 'The people rejoiced as the new emperor took the throne. They put up white banners like clouds and held rites in remembrance of the previous emperor's virtue. But their hearts were hopeful, confident that an era of greatness was upon us, and the nation's peace secure...'
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: This is from a century ago, when the last True Lung took the throne.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: That's a long time ago. Does it have anything to do with what we're investigating?
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: The last True Lung's accomplishments were innumerable. The transformation of Baizao into a nomadic city, as well as the Sky Mirror Pavilion, were completed under his decree.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Not that either of us were around back then... although I suppose he would be your grandfather.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Look at this foreword... talk about sucking up to the powerful.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: The county records from around Baizao and the demographic records of Baizao itself all paint a picture that is far less rosy.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: In the end, only one person's accomplishments remain.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Same old story no matter where you are, I guess.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: That is kind of strange to hear from a close blood relative of that emperor.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: 'When the city of Baizao was completed, the emperor ordered the tomes of all the archives to be gathered in a single place.'
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: 'The scholars worked by candlelight, collecting fragments and tales, validating the facts and compiling them, so that the scattered records are whole again.'
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: That's strange. The Imperial Historical Cabinet's efforts to collect the scattered histories happened after the death of the proxy, more than a decade before Baizao was built.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: But the ambiguous way that it's written... the older generations were always stringent about scholarship. Why would they make such a mistake?
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Could it be that... even what little they left behind was altered and rewritten?
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Ch'en Hui-chieh is not listening to her companion's muttering. She is on guard for any unusual movement around them.
She wanted to tell Ch'en Chao-ch'ien to get moving, but she had a hard time saying it, seeing the sad look on Ch'en Chao-ch'ien's face.
I never asked you... what happened to your family? The ones who were in Baizao?
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: The Ch'ens were a family of historians. They were well-regarded but not illustrious. They generally stayed out of conflict and politics, other than the occasional offense they inevitably caused in the course of their work as historians.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: All I remember is that my father said it was as though the sky had fallen when the family learned about the marriage arrangement with the princess.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: That cousin was the only unmarried man in the family. He, and everyone else, knew how dangerous it was, but they were in no position to refuse...
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: The rest was history. Princess Yanjing died in Lungmen, and my cousin passed away soon after.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: His Imperial Majesty did not punish our family, but rumors of strife took on a life of their own. You can imagine what happened to us when everyone decided our house had mistreated the princess.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: That's not what Mother wanted...
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: ...I doubt it was Wei Yenwu's intention either.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: I was very young then. All I saw was...
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: The elders were always sighing when they came back from the morning assembly. Many fell ill out of sheer worry. Brothers and sisters stopped communicating with one another...
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Everything was shrouded in gloom, but I didn't know why.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Father moved from place to place, selling off the family's belongings, until we finally left Baizao altogether, yet he never once told me the reason.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: As for what happened after... you know, there was a time, after becoming Magistrate of Water here in the Sky Mirror Pavilion, when I thought I couldn't let the family tradition of history die with me.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: I didn't complain about the job. Most of the position's responsibilities had been replaced by machines anyway; my colleagues, while not the friendliest lot, had little interest in political backstabbing.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: I had time on my hands, so I thought I would start with my family's history.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Was it because the Magistrate of Water's rank was too low? I felt like I had stumbled into a thick fog, getting more lost with every step I took.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: I knew my family didn't do anything wrong, but all the records I could find denounced us for our arrogance, our abuses.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: I tracked down the ones who were still alive, tried to ask them what they knew. But they couldn't get away fast enough when they learned I'm of the Ch'en family, and the 'Chao' generation.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: The few who were willing to see me said a bunch of apologetic words in officialese, but refused to tell me anything about the truth of what happened...
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: I spent ten years on this until I even started to believe it myself, that the House of Ch'en deserved what it got for mistreating the princess!
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: But I know... I know it wasn't true!
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: What am I? What is the House of Ch'en? What were your dead parents?
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: What is history?
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: All I know is that history should be a record of the truth. That's what justice means to me.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Duoxie.
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: As for the family history you wanted to know...
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: To tell the truth... my parents died when I was very young. I don't remember much about them.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: And Wei Yenwu was... Wei Yenwu. It's hard for me to explain to people who my parents were. Sometimes I pass off the image of Wei Yenwu as my father's, just to make things simple.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: I don't ever remember my mother smiling. And I don't remember many positive things about my father.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: I just can't imagine how it feels... to have your life determined entirely by other people, by those baseless things like responsibility and honor.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: It's not fair to you... and it's not fair to them.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Is life fair and just to anyone?
attributes.name: Ch'en Chao-ch'ien

attributes.content: Someone once said to me that the justice we seek is not in the past... but that doesn't mean we should forget about it.
attributes.name: Ch'en
